---
title: Comparing Centered vs. Non-Centered Parameterizations
jupyter:
  jupytext:
    text_representation:
      extension: .qmd
      format_name: quarto
      format_version: '1.0'
      jupytext_version: 1.19.1
  kernelspec:
    display_name: renewal_model_dj
    language: python
    name: renewal_model_dj
---

```{python}
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import arviz as az
from arviz_base import from_cmdstanpy
import numpy as np
import plotnine as p9
import polars as pl
import pandas as pd
from cmdstanpy import CmdStanMCMC, CmdStanModel

np.set_printoptions(suppress=True)
pd.set_option('display.float_format', '{:.4f}'.format)
```

```{python}
def plot_rt_posterior(
    rt,
    true_rt=None,
    interval: float = 0.90,
    title: str = "Posterior Rt with 90% interval",
):
    alpha = (1 - interval) / 2
    q_low = alpha
    q_high = 1 - alpha

    time = rt.coords["time"].values

    rt_df = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "day": time,
            "median": rt.quantile(0.50, dim=["chain", "draw"]).values,
            "q_low": rt.quantile(q_low, dim=["chain", "draw"]).values,
            "q_high": rt.quantile(q_high, dim=["chain", "draw"]).values,
        }
    )

    plot = (
        p9.ggplot(rt_df, p9.aes(x="day"))
        + p9.geom_ribbon(
            p9.aes(ymin="q_low", ymax="q_high"),
            fill="steelblue",
            alpha=0.25,
        )
        + p9.geom_line(
            p9.aes(y="median"),
            color="darkblue",
            size=1,
        )
        + p9.labs(x="Day", y="Rt", title=title)
    )

    if true_rt is not None:
        true_rt_df = pd.DataFrame({"day": time, "true_Rt": true_rt})
        plot = plot + p9.geom_line(
            data=true_rt_df,
            mapping=p9.aes(x="day", y="true_Rt"),
            color="black",
            linetype="dashed",
            size=0.8,
        )

    return plot
```

### Load Datasets

Load synthetic data, assemble inputs into data dictionary passed to CmdStanModel's `sample` method.

```{python}
def load_data(data_dir: Path, signal_name: str = "ed_visits") -> dict:
    """Load synthetic data files and assemble the Stan data dictionary."""
    with open(data_dir / "true_parameters.json") as f:
        params = json.load(f)
    with open(Path(params["signal_config_file"])) as f:
        signal_config = json.load(f)

    signal = next(s for s in signal_config["signals"] if s["name"] == signal_name)
    observations = pl.read_csv(data_dir / params["signal_outputs"][signal_name])

    w: list[float] = params["generation_interval_pmf"]
    pi: list[float] = signal["delay_pmf"]
    S = len(w)
    D = len(pi)
    L = max(D, S)
    T = len(observations)

    return {
        "T": T,
        "S": S,
        "D": D,
        "y": observations["observed_count"].to_list(),
        "w": w,
        "pi": pi,
        "J": params["pre_observation_infections"][-L:],
        "true_Rt": params["true_rt"],
    }
```

```{python}
stan_data_84 = load_data(Path("data/synth_hosp_ed_84"))
stan_data_126 = load_data(Path("data/synth_hosp_ed_126"))
stan_data_189 = load_data(Path("data/synth_hosp_ed_189"))
```

### Compile models

We compile the cenetered and non-centered parameterizations.

#### Centered parameterization

```stan
// simple renewal model
// infer rt,  ascertainment rate, and overdispersion
// random walk prior on time-varying renewal process
// one source of observations
// fixed estimates for infection, observational delay distributions
// initial infections provided as data
data {
  // observational data
  int<lower=0> T;                // total days
  int<lower=0> S;                // generation interval length
  int<lower=0> D;                // delay distribution length
  array[T] int<lower=0> y;       // observed events
  simplex[S] w;                  // generation interval
  simplex[D] pi;                 // delay distribution
  vector<lower=0>[max(D, S)] J;  // infections at time t < 0
}
transformed data {
  int<lower=0> L = max(D, S);      // max lookback
  simplex[S] w_rev = reverse(w);   // rearrange for convolution
  simplex[D] pi_rev = reverse(pi);
}
parameters {
  vector[T] log_r;                // time-varying reproduction number (log scale)
  real<lower=0, upper=1> alpha;   // ascertainment rate
  real<lower=0> inv_sqrt_phi;     // 0 = Poisson; 1 = heavy overdispersion
  real<lower=0> sigma_rw;         // temporal smoothness
}
transformed parameters {
  real<lower=0> phi = inv(square(inv_sqrt_phi));
}
model {
  vector[T] mu;
  vector[L + T] I;
  I[1:L] = J;
  for (t in 1:T) {
    int lpt = L + t;
    I[lpt] = exp(log_r[t]) * dot_product(I[lpt - S:lpt - 1], w_rev);
    mu[t] = alpha * dot_product(I[lpt - D + 1:lpt], pi_rev);
  }
  y ~ neg_binomial_2(mu, phi);

  // priors on all parameters
  alpha ~ beta(1, 100);
  inv_sqrt_phi ~ normal(0, 1);
  log_r[1] ~ normal(0, 0.5);          // initial R centered at 1
  log_r[2:T] ~ normal(log_r[1:T-1], sigma_rw);  // RW1 prior on r
  sigma_rw ~ normal(0, 0.1);
}
generated quantities {
  vector[T] r = exp(log_r);
}
```

#### Non-centered parameterization

The `data` and `transformed data` blocks are the same.

```stan
transformed parameters {
  real<lower=0> phi = inv(square(inv_sqrt_phi));
}
model {
  // Reconstruct log R(t) from initial level + scaled cumulative innovations.
  vector[T] log_r;
  log_r[1] = log_r_init;
  log_r[2:T] = log_r_init + sigma_rw * cumulative_sum(z);

  vector[T] mu;
  vector[L + T] I;
  I[1:L] = J;
  for (t in 1:T) {
    int lpt = L + t;
    I[lpt] = exp(log_r[t]) * dot_product(I[lpt - S:lpt - 1], w_rev);
    mu[t] = alpha * dot_product(I[lpt - D + 1:lpt], pi_rev);
  }
  y ~ neg_binomial_2(mu, phi);

  // Priors
  alpha ~ beta(1, 100);           // low ascertainment rate
  inv_sqrt_phi ~ normal(0, 1);
  log_r_init ~ normal(0, 0.5);    // initial R centered at 1
  z ~ std_normal();        
  sigma_rw ~ normal(0, 0.5);      // half-normal
}
generated quantities {
  vector[T] r;
  {
    vector[T] log_r;
    log_r[1] = log_r_init;
    log_r[2:T] = log_r_init + sigma_rw * cumulative_sum(z);
    r = exp(log_r);
  }
}```

(note - we compute log_r twice - faster than doing this once in transformed parameters because of I/O overhead)

```{python}
cp_stan_file = Path("stan/simple_rw_3p.stan")
cp = CmdStanModel(stan_file=cp_stan_file)

ncp_stan_file = Path("stan/simple_rw_3p_nc.stan")
ncp = CmdStanModel(stan_file=ncp_stan_file)
```

#### 84 days (12 weeks)

Fit the centered parameterization

```{python}
cp_84_fit = cp.sample(
        data=stan_data_84,
        chains=4,
        parallel_chains=4,
        seed=42,
        iter_sampling=2000,
    )
```

Fit the non-centered parameterization

```{python}
ncp_84_fit = ncp.sample(
    data=stan_data_84,
    chains=4,
    parallel_chains=4,
    seed=42,
    max_treedepth=12,
)
```

Inspect estimates via ArviZ 1.0.
Create an `idata` object that contains posterior samples as well as observational data.

```{python}
time_84 = np.arange(stan_data_84["T"])
cp_84_idata = from_cmdstanpy(
    posterior=cp_84_fit,
    coords={"time": time_84},
    dims={"r": ["time"],"log_r": ["time"]},
    observed_data={
        "ed_visits": np.asarray(stan_data_84["y"]),
        "true_Rt": np.asarray(stan_data_84["true_Rt"]),
    },
)
az.summary(cp_84_idata, var_names=["alpha", "phi", "sigma_rw", "r"])
```

```{python}
ncp_84_idata = from_cmdstanpy(
    posterior=ncp_84_fit,
    coords={"time": time_84},
    dims={"r": ["time"],"log_r": ["time"]},
    observed_data={
        "ed_visits": np.asarray(stan_data_84["y"]),
        "true_Rt": np.asarray(stan_data_84["true_Rt"]),
    },
)
az.summary(ncp_84_idata, var_names=["alpha", "phi", "sigma_rw", "r"])
```

```{python}
plot_rt_posterior(cp_84_idata.posterior["r"], stan_data_84["true_Rt"])
```

```{python}
plot_rt_posterior(ncp_84_idata.posterior["r"], stan_data_84["true_Rt"])
```

### 126 days (18 weeks)

```{python}
cp_126_fit = cp.sample(
        data=stan_data_126,
        chains=4,
        parallel_chains=4,
        seed=42,
        iter_sampling=3000
    )
```

```{python}
ncp_126_fit = ncp.sample(
        data=stan_data_126,
        chains=4,
        parallel_chains=4,
        seed=42,
)
```

```{python}
time_126 = np.arange(stan_data_126["T"])
cp_126_idata = from_cmdstanpy(
    posterior=cp_126_fit,
    coords={"time": time_126},
    dims={"r": ["time"],"log_r": ["time"]},
    observed_data={
        "ed_visits": np.asarray(stan_data_126["y"]),
        "true_Rt": np.asarray(stan_data_126["true_Rt"]),
    },
)
az.summary(cp_126_idata, var_names=["alpha", "phi", "sigma_rw", "r"])
```

```{python}
ncp_126_idata = from_cmdstanpy(
    posterior=ncp_126_fit,
    coords={"time": time_126},
    dims={"r": ["time"],"log_r": ["time"]},
    observed_data={
        "ed_visits": np.asarray(stan_data_126["y"]),
        "true_Rt": np.asarray(stan_data_126["true_Rt"]),
    },
)
az.summary(ncp_126_idata, var_names=["alpha", "phi", "sigma_rw", "r"])
```

```{python}
plot_rt_posterior(cp_126_idata.posterior["r"], stan_data_126["true_Rt"])
```

```{python}
plot_rt_posterior(ncp_126_idata.posterior["r"], stan_data_126["true_Rt"])
```

### 189 days (27 weeks)

```{python}
cp_189_fit = cp.sample(
        data=stan_data_189,
        chains=4,
        parallel_chains=4,
        seed=42,
        iter_sampling=2000,
    )
```

```{python}
ncp_189_fit = ncp.sample(
        data=stan_data_189,
        chains=4,
        parallel_chains=4,
        seed=42,
        iter_sampling=2000,
    )
```

```{python}
time_189 = np.arange(stan_data_189["T"])
cp_189_idata = from_cmdstanpy(
    posterior=cp_189_fit,
    coords={"time": time_189},
    dims={"r": ["time"],"log_r": ["time"]},
    observed_data={
        "ed_visits": np.asarray(stan_data_189["y"]),
        "true_Rt": np.asarray(stan_data_189["true_Rt"]),
    },
)
az.summary(cp_189_idata, var_names=["alpha", "phi", "sigma_rw", "r"])
```

```{python}
ncp_189_idata = from_cmdstanpy(
    posterior=ncp_189_fit,
    coords={"time": time_189},
    dims={"r": ["time"],"log_r": ["time"]},
    observed_data={
        "ed_visits": np.asarray(stan_data_189["y"]),
        "true_Rt": np.asarray(stan_data_189["true_Rt"]),
    },
)
az.summary(ncp_189_idata, var_names=["alpha", "phi", "sigma_rw", "r"])
```

```{python}
plot_rt_posterior(cp_189_idata.posterior["r"], true_rt=stan_data_189["true_Rt"])
```

```{python}
plot_rt_posterior(ncp_189_idata.posterior["r"], true_rt=stan_data_189["true_Rt"])
```
